data.narrative:
Bohr Systems is a pre-seed stage Defense & Security company founded by Rohil Gharat (former Cisco, Antler, Founders Inc.) and Samarth Kumbla (Columbia CS '27). The broader sector comprises 19 known peers; the relevant comp universe includes 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with top-tier private comparables including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The dataset contains zero pre-seed or adjacent-stage Defense & Security companies with sufficient data density to establish a reliable narrow-band comparable set. While 19 same-sector peers exist, filtering for matching stage and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for defensible median-based inference. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the capital intensity and regulatory requirements unique to Defense & Security.

Without a sufficiently dense peer set at this stage, traditional triangulation cannot be completed. Valuation support would require alternative approaches: detailed financial modeling (currently constrained by pre-revenue status), venture capital method calibrated to the sector's typical expansion multiples and exit outcomes, or reliance on founder pedigree and team composition as signals of future fundraising power. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within a crowded subsector, regulatory pathway clarity, and customer traction velocity. A pre-seed round in this sector typically reflects founder credibility and market thesis rather than quantifiable financial metrics.
